note: Webform has a large surface (config entity + submissions, 5 plugin types, drush commands, submodules, permissions). Cases span three difficulty tiers: easy (recipe, response-graded, no site mutation), medium (introspection — a setup script installs a known `eval_intro` webform and the agent must inspect the live site to answer), and hard (execution — the agent must build a webform on the live site and a verify script checks it). Recipe cases probe load-bearing, webform-specific facts an agent would otherwise dig through source to answer.

evals.prompt: In Drupal Webform, what kind of entity is a form itself versus a single response/submission? Name each entity type and whether it is config or content.
evals.expected_output: The form is a `webform` config entity (WebformInterface); each response is a `webform_submission` content entity (WebformSubmissionInterface).

evals.prompt: I want to write a custom handler that runs when a Webform submission is saved (to POST it to Slack). What plugin type, annotation, base class, and source directory do I use in my module?
evals.expected_output: A WebformHandler plugin: `@WebformHandler` annotation, extend `Drupal\webform\Plugin\WebformHandlerBase`, placed in `src/Plugin/WebformHandler/`, e.g. override postSave().

evals.prompt: List the plugin types that the Webform module defines for developers to extend.
evals.expected_output: Five plugin types: WebformElement, WebformHandler, WebformExporter, WebformVariant, WebformSourceEntity.

evals.prompt: Give the Drush command to export a Webform's results to a file from the command line, and the command to create test submissions for a form.
evals.expected_output: `drush webform:export <webform_id>` to export results; `drush webform:generate <webform_id> [num]` to create test submissions.

evals.prompt: On a Webform site I can only edit forms via the raw YAML source. Which submodule provides the drag-and-drop element builder UI so editors can add elements through forms instead of YAML?
evals.expected_output: The `webform_ui` submodule provides the drag-and-drop element builder UI.

evals.prompt: Which Webform permission machine name controls access to the raw YAML Source editor for editing a form's elements tree?
evals.expected_output: The `edit webform source` permission gates the raw YAML Source editor.

evals.prompt: In a Webform YAML element definition, how do I show a `phone` field only when a `wants_call` checkbox is checked, without writing code? Show the element property to use.
evals.expected_output: Use the `#states` property for conditional logic, e.g. `'#states': { visible: { ':input[name="wants_call"]': { checked: true } } }`.

evals.prompt: Which built-in Webform handler sends a notification/confirmation email on submission, and which one POSTs the submission data to an external URL (CRM/webhook)?
evals.expected_output: EmailWebformHandler (id `email`) sends notification/confirmation email; RemotePostWebformHandler (id `remote_post`) POSTs submission data to an external URL.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), build a working contact webform with machine name `eval_contact`. It must have three required elements — a full name (textfield), an email address (email element), and a message (textarea) — and it must email each submission to team@example.com via a webform email handler. Apply it to the live site so the form actually exists and works; do not just describe it. Give a one-line summary.
evals.expected_output: An eval_contact webform with name/email/message required elements + an email handler exists on the live site.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site already has a webform with machine name `eval_intro` configured. Inspect the live site (drush is available) and tell me: what confirmation message does it show submitters after they submit? Quote it exactly.
must_contain_any: Thank you — your enquiry reference is ENQ-4815.
evals.expected_output: Its confirmation message is: "Thank you — your enquiry reference is ENQ-4815."

evals.prompt: This Drupal site already has a webform with machine name `eval_intro` configured. Inspect the live site (drush is available) and list which elements it defines and which of them are required.
evals.expected_output: eval_intro defines four elements: full_name (textfield, required), email (email, required), department (select, required) and subscribe (checkbox, optional/not required).

evals.prompt: This Drupal site already has a webform with machine name `eval_intro` configured. Inspect the live site (drush is available) and tell me what recipient email address its email handler sends submission notifications to.
evals.expected_output: Its email handler sends notifications to intake@eval.example.org.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), build a working survey webform with machine name `eval_survey`. It must contain a required `select` element for a satisfaction rating whose options are exactly the keys `poor` (Poor), `good` (Good) and `excellent` (Excellent), plus a `checkbox` element (e.g. "Contact me about my feedback"). Apply it to the live site so the form actually exists and works; do not just describe it. Give a one-line summary.
evals.expected_output: An eval_survey webform with a required select element (options poor/good/excellent) and a checkbox element exists on the live site.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), build a working signup webform with machine name `eval_signup` (a couple of simple elements are fine). Configure its submission confirmation so that instead of showing a message it redirects the submitter to the site front page (confirmation type = URL, confirmation URL = `<front>`), and add a webform email handler that notifies someone on each submission. Apply it to the live site so the form actually exists and works; do not just describe it. Give a one-line summary.
evals.expected_output: An eval_signup webform whose confirmation redirects to <front> (confirmation_type url) and that has an email handler exists on the live site.
